title: What do you think future Shinji and Asuka would tell their younger selves
display_title: What do you think future Shinji and Asuka would tell their younger selves
wikitext:
[]: What do you think future Shinji and Asuka would tell their younger selves?             Quote Post by C.T.1290 » Tue Jul 16, 2024 6:07 pm This is basically one of those what ifs scenarios, where supposedly Shinji and Asuka got to meet their past selves. Do you think they would offer them some advice or give them the fair warnings about their future? Shinji would probably tell his past self to stop running away from things and hopes he doesn’t start Third Impact, Asuka would most likely tell her younger self to not push people away so much and be more honest with herself. Those are my two cents, anyway.

[]: Re: What do you think future Shinji and Asuka would tell their younger selves?             Quote Post by Gendos Glasses » Thu Jul 18, 2024 6:32 pm Asuka would maybe say that her mother is part of her EVA

[]: Re: What do you think future Shinji and Asuka would tell their younger selves?             Quote Post by silentleviathan » Fri Jul 26, 2024 3:06 pm To me, the first answer that comes into my head is "Holy shit, talk to each other, please." but I realize there's a reason why they don't. The whole show is a journey explaining why they don't and why they have the barriers that they do. I think their future selves (assuming post instrumentality where they've resolved their issues) would have to do a lot of talking to their past selves to get them to a point where they would accept any advice at all.

Do you think they would offer them some advice or give them the fair warnings about their future? Shinji would probably tell his past self to stop running away from things and hopes he do

Do you think they would offer them some advice or give them the fair warnings about their future? Shinji would probably tell his past self to stop running away from things and hopes he doesn’t start Third Impact, Asuka would most likely tell her younger self to not push people away so much and be more honest with herself. Those are my two cents, anyway.

[]: Re: What do you think future Shinji and Asuka would tell their younger selves?             Quote Post by silentleviathan » Fri Jul 26, 2024 3:06 pm To me, the first answer that comes into my head is "Holy shit, talk to each other, please." but I realize there's a reason why they don't. The whole show is a journey explaining why they don't and why they have the barriers that they do. I think their future selves (assuming post instrumentality where they've resolved their issues) would have to do a lot of talking to their past selves to get them to a point where they would accept any advice at all.
